prose:
Frontier-orbital (HOMO–LUMO) gap of formaldehyde

Method provenance
• Property: closed-shell HOMO–LUMO gap (Koopmans orbital energies), eV
• Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` → B3LYP/def2-TZVP with RI (density fitting) in PySCF 2.x 
• Geometry provenance: fixed experimental geometry supplied as XYZ at /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz; single-point calculation only (no re-optimization)
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation)
• Charge = 0, multiplicity = 1 (restricted singlet)
• Software: PySCF via chemkit `frontier-orbitals` skill (CLI invocation logged below)
• SCF convergence: converged in 8 cycles; gradient information not applicable (single point)
• Warnings: no warnings reported by engine
• Live .out log (streamed during run): /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp1w4jxo3e/live.json.out (path echoed at job start)

Result
HOMO energy = –7.6523067 eV
LUMO energy = –1.7826355 eV
→ HOMO–LUMO gap = 5.8696712 eV (headline value)

The calculation was carried out with the chemkit `frontier-orbitals` skill using B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier standard, RI) in the gas phase. The engine’s integrity check reports status "ok" and trustworthy = true.

Command executed (reproducible):
cli.py frontier --method dft --tier standard --charge 0 --mult 1 /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz --out formaldehyde_gap.json
